title: Farah Pandith on Office Hours Podcast
summary: Farah Pandith (@Farah_Pandith), Senior Fellow at the Belfer Center’s Future of Diplomacy Project and formerly the first ever Special Representative to Muslim communities at the U.S. State Department, sits down with Aroop Mukharji (@aroopmukharji) to talk about how to curb the spread of violent extremism, particularly among Muslim youth.
body: Farah Pandith is a diplomatic entrepreneur and foreign policy strategist. In addition to serving as an adjunct senior fellow at the Council on Foreign Relations and a senior fellow at Harvard University’s Kennedy School of Government, she is currently a member of Secretary Jeh Johnson’s Homeland Security Advisory Council (HSAC) and chairs its subcommittee on countering violent extremism (CVE). She is also a Commissioner and Strategic Advisor on the Center for Strategic and International Studies CVE Commission. She focuses on driving efforts to counter extremism through new organizations, programs and initiatives, most notably Halcyon, a new innovative global organization she has co-founded dedicated to mobilizing youth against extremist ideologies. Ms. Pandith has served as a political appointee in the George H.W. Bush, George W. Bush, and Barack H. Obama administrations. Ms. Pandith was appointed the first-ever special representative to Muslim Communities in June 2009 by Secretary of State Hillary Rodham Clinton, serving under both Secretaries Clinton and John Kerry. The Office of the Special Representative was responsible for engaging with Muslims around the world both organizationally and individually. In January 2013, she was awarded the Secretary's Distinguished Honor Award. _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: Farah Pandith is a diplomatic entrepreneur and foreign policy strategist holding significant roles in countering violent extremism (CVE). She serves as an adjunct senior fellow at the Council on Foreign Relations and a senior fellow at Harvard University's Kennedy School of Government. Her current official appointments include membership on Secretary Jeh Johnson's Homeland Security Advisory Council (HSAC), where she chairs its CVE subcommittee, and a role as Commissioner and Strategic Advisor on the Center for Strategic and International Studies CVE Commission. Her work focuses on creating new organizations, programs, and initiatives to counter extremism, notably co-founding Halcyon, an innovative global organization dedicated to mobilizing youth against extremist ideologies. Pandith has a history of political appointments across the George H.W. Bush, George W. Bush, and Barack Obama administrations. A landmark role was her appointment in June 2009 by Secretary of State Hillary Rodham Clinton as the first-ever Special Representative to Muslim Communities, a position she held under both Secretaries Clinton and John Kerry. In this capacity, she was responsible for U.S. engagement with Muslims worldwide. Her service was recognized in January 2013 with the Secretary's Distinguished Honor Award.
result.main_content_zh: 法拉·潘迪斯是一位外交企业家和外交政策战略家，在打击暴力极端主义领域担任重要职务。她是美国对外关系委员会的兼职高级研究员和哈佛大学肯尼迪政府学院的高级研究员。她目前的官方任命包括担任杰·约翰逊部长国土安全咨询委员会的成员并领导其打击暴力极端主义小组委员会，同时担任战略与国际研究中心打击暴力极端主义委员会的委员和战略顾问。她的工作重点是创建新的组织、计划和倡议以打击极端主义，特别是共同创立了Halcyon——一个致力于动员青年对抗极端主义意识形态的创新性全球组织。潘迪斯曾在乔治·H·W·布什、乔治·W·布什和巴拉克·奥巴马政府中担任政治任命职务。其标志性角色是于2009年6月由国务卿希拉里·罗德姆·克林顿任命为首任穆斯林社区特别代表，并在克林顿和约翰·克里两位国务卿任内履职。在此职位上，她负责美国与全球穆斯林的接触事务。她的服务在2013年1月获得了国务卿杰出荣誉奖的认可。
keywords_en: Center for Strategic and International Studies
